Lamentablemente, mi experiencia en Terra Cotta fue humillante y totalmente inaceptable. Fuimos con la ilusión de pasar un buen rato en un grupo de 6 personas, pero desde el momento en que nos acercamos a la entrada, el trato del personal fue pésimo. Al solicitar la mesa, nos atendieron de una manera déspota y despectiva. Nos dijeron tajantemente que "no tenían lugar" y que tendríamos que esperar muchísimo tiempo para poder pasar. Esto fue un insulto a nuestra inteligencia, ya que claramente podíamos ver mesas vacías y disponibles en ese momento. Lo peor no fue la negativa, sino la actitud. El personal nos barrió con la mirada de arriba a abajo con un desdén increíble, haciéndonos sentir que no éramos bienvenidos. Fue evidente que el trato hostil se debió a que somos nacionales; notamos que el restaurante estaba lleno exclusivamente de extranjeros, quienes recibían una atención muy diferente. Preferimos retirarnos dignamente antes que quedarnos a que nos siguieran humillando o nos hicieran el "favor" de atendernos. Es una pena que en nuestro propio país existan lugares que practiquen este tipo de discriminación. No pienso volver y definitivamente no lo recomiendo a nadie que espere un trato respetuoso e igualitario. Unfortunately, my experience at Terra Cotta was humiliating and completely unacceptable. We went hoping to have a good time as a group of 6, but from the moment we approached the entrance, the staff’s treatment was awful. When we asked for a table, we were met with a despotic and derogatory attitude. They flatly told us they "didn't have room" and that we would have to wait a very long time to be seated. This was an insult to our intelligence, as we could clearly see empty tables available right then. The worst part wasn't just the refusal, but the attitude. The staff looked us up and down with incredible disdain, making us feel unwelcome. It was obvious that this hostile treatment was because we are locals; we noticed the restaurant was filled exclusively with foreigners who were receiving very different service. We preferred to leave with dignity rather than stay to be humiliated further or have them do us the "favor" of serving us. It is a shame that places practicing this type of discrimination exist in our own country. I do not plan to return, and I definitely do not recommend this place to anyone expecting respectful and equal treatment.

I run an animal rescue in Mexico and have visited many places here, but this was one of the worst experiences. My girlfriend and I were excited to celebrate my birthday weekend at this beautiful beach. However, when we arrived around midday, we were shocked to be turned away. Three employees claimed they were closed and mentioned their closing time was 5 pm, which should have given us plenty of time. But to our surprise, they abruptly stated that they were at capacity. During our conversation, we witnessed at least 10 cars full of people leaving the area. I firmly expressed my disagreement with them being at capacity, but they were extremely rude and seemed to be on a power trip. They flatly refused to allow us access to the beach, even though we had driven over five hours to get there. The most distressing part was the suspicion of racism. If you are American, be cautious, as it felt like they treated us unfairly due to our nationality. It's disappointing to encounter such unwelcoming and inhospitable behavior, especially in a place that should be a paradise for all visitors. Soy dueño de un refugio de animales en México y he visitado muchos lugares aquí, pero esta fue una de las peores experiencias. Mi novia y yo estábamos emocionados por celebrar mi fin de semana de cumpleaños en esta hermosa playa. Sin embargo, cuando llegamos alrededor del mediodía, nos sorprendió que nos negaran la entrada. Tres empleados afirmaron que estaban cerrados y mencionaron que su hora de cierre era a las 5 pm, lo que debería habernos dado tiempo de sobra. Pero para nuestra sorpresa, afirmaron de manera brusca que estaban a capacidad. Durante nuestra conversación, presenciamos al menos 10 autos llenos de personas abandonando la zona. Expresé firmemente mi desacuerdo con respecto a su capacidad, pero fueron extremadamente groseros y parecían estar abusando de su autoridad. Rotundamente se negaron a permitirnos el acceso a la playa, a pesar de haber conducido más de cinco horas para llegar allí. La parte más angustiante fue la sospecha de racismo. Si eres estadounidense, ten cuidado, ya que sentimos que nos trataron de manera injusta debido a nuestra nacionalidad. Es decepcionante encontrarse con un comportamiento tan inhóspito y poco acogedor, especialmente en un lugar que debería ser un paraíso para todos los visitantes.

un museo, regionale a misura di visita, nel senso abbastanza rapido. una sala al piano terra con foto e riproduzioni di animali e luoghi della baja california sur. primo piano storia etno-antropologica della penisola. secondo piano inizio epoca moderna e colonizzazione. terzo piano da fine 800 alla rivoluzione di benito juarez e successive fino allo statuto e alla carta di sito protetto unesco in quanto definito da jacques cousteau "acquario del mondo". tutta la penisola é luogo fatto di spiagge, deserti rocciosi, colline e montagne canion e la polvere immancabile. vale la pena visitarlo, permanenza 1 ora e mezza. sulle immagini o sulle reliquie sono posti i codici qr per continuare la visita virtuale altrove. ingresso gratuito per studenti con tesserino di riconoscimento, pensionati over 60 ovviamente messicani. stranieri 70 pesos messicani.
